3.happening: Count the elements — {n} in the array.
3.why: We scan every one of them.

4.happening: Seed the running minimum with the first element ({nums[0]}).
4.why: We need a starting candidate before scanning.
4.invariant: ans is the smallest element seen so far.

5.why: We walk the array left to right.
5.invariant: Elements before i have all been compared.

6.happening: There are still elements to check — continue the scan.
6.why: Every element must be inspected in a linear scan.
6.invariant: ans is the minimum of nums[0..i-1].

6.happening: All elements checked — the scan is done.
6.why: i has passed the last index.

7.happening: nums[{i}] = {nums[i]} is smaller than the running minimum {ans}.
7.why: A smaller value means we found a new minimum.

7.happening: nums[{i}] = {nums[i]} is not smaller than {ans} — keep the current minimum.
7.why: This element can't lower the minimum.

8.happening: Lower the running minimum to nums[{i}] = {nums[i]}.
8.why: It's the smallest value seen so far.
8.invariant: ans now equals the smallest of nums[0..i].

init.happening: Seeding the running minimum and the scan index.
init.why: We need a starting candidate and position.
init.invariant: ans starts as the first element.

loop.happening: Scanning the array element by element.
loop.why: A linear scan inspects every value.
loop.invariant: ans is the minimum of the prefix scanned so far.

check.happening: Comparing the current element to the running minimum.
check.why: Only a smaller value updates the answer.
check.invariant: ans holds the smallest prefix value.

update.why: A new smallest value was found.
update.invariant: ans equals the smallest value seen.

move.why: The scan proceeds left to right.

return.why: The whole array has been scanned.
